other_observers: None during the initial sighting.; I am the original finder.
specific_location: Chico Basin Ranch Upper Twin Ponds - South-west corner.
description: A small flat-headed sparrow with orange-yellow on the supercillium, supraloral, throat, side of neck, breast and sides. It had a dark lateral crown stripe with a light gray median crown stripe. It had a dark postocular stripe and moustachial stripe. It was gray on the upper back and on the ear coverts and a narrow strip of gray on the sides. It had a cleanly separated white belly. It had feint dark streaks on the sides and breast.Behaviors: The bird was hidden in the bullrushes on the edge of the pond. When I approached it popped up into plain view for a few seconds and then flew about 15 feet and remained out of sight. A few minutes later when I returned to the area it repeated this behavior. I never heard it make a sound.Call: bird was silentPlumage: unknown
photographer: Photographer: Robb Hinds, Robb Hinds, Robb Hinds
